objects.name: Analytic 1706
objects.description: Defender observes an app (package/UID) repeatedly retrieving network interface configuration attributes (local IP/MAC/interface names, active network capabilities, link properties, proxy/DNS settings, or carrier identifiers when permitted) in a short time window, without corresponding user network-management activity. The pattern is characterized by OS API execution for interface/config reads combined with background state, permission/role context (e.g., device owner/profile owner/carrier/default-SMS), and optional follow-on connectivity tests (gateway/DNS/proxy reachability). Correlate across API execution + app state + (optional) local probe to identify automated network configuration discovery rather than routine connectivity checks.

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Window to correlate config reads with app state and optional connectivity tests (e.g., 30–300s).
Minimum number of network-config read signals before flagging (environment dependent; e.g., ≥10/5m).
If true, require the app to be backgrounded to reduce legitimate network UI/diagnostic activity.
Connectivity/security/MDM apps expected to query network configuration frequently.
If true, elevate severity when an app with device-owner/profile-owner/carrier roles performs bursts.
Ports considered 'connectivity tests' (e.g., 53, 80, 443, 8080, 3128) – tune per environment.
Suppress alerts shortly after legitimate network transitions (Wi-Fi join, VPN connect) to reduce noise.
